proposed_from.topic: Flagship partial-identification upgrade for factorial difference-in-differences: characterize the sharp identified set for causal moderation in a finite 2x2 factorial DiD design when the standard FDID estimand identifies only effect modification, allowing a bounded imbalance budget between moderator strata in untreated trends. Recover the recent factorial-DID point-identification result at zero imbalance, give an exact finite-cell LP/dual interval for moderation under nonzero imbalance, and exhibit a concrete witness where effect modification and causal moderation have opposite signs.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-repo object remains pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1, whose kernel is a sharp support interval for bounded latent DID anticipation distortions in staggered ATT problems. The present proposal differs in three ways: it targets FDID causal moderation rather than ATT, its nuisance is a moderator-stratum untreated-trend quotient tied to the Xu-Zhao-Ding moderation/effect-modification gap, and its flagship claim is a projection/lift theorem from a full six-coordinate completion Gamma(P;rho) to a two-coordinate FDID quotient body E(P;rho). The banked warnings in panel_honest_did_phase_v1 and pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 motivate the shared-coordinate geometry, but neither supplies a factorial-moderation completion map or an opposite-sign witness.","published_axis":"XuZhaoDing2026FDID is the benchmark point-identification paper at zero imbalance, not the novelty claim. RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends gives the partial-ID sensitivity template, and BalkePearl1997Bounds plus Demuynck2015LPBounds give the finite LP technology, but none instantiates the FDID causal-moderation target with moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance coordinates and none gives a finite-cell opposite-sign witness. The epsilon-gap is therefore the FDID-specific completion/projection object: a sharp identified set for causal moderation under positive imbalance budgets that collapses to Xu-Zhao-Ding at zero budget and yields an exact support frontier min{rho_*, rho_0+rho_1} for sign reversal."}
seed_list: Derive the sharp finite-cell LP and dual support interval for FDID causal moderation when factorial parallel trends is relaxed to a bounded moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance budget, collapsing to XuZhaoDing2026FDID at zero imbalance.

Define the critical imbalance scalar rho^-(P) for FDID and characterize exactly when a positive effect-modification contrast can coexist with negative causal moderation on an open class of finite tables.

Give a necessary-and-sufficient equality certificate for when an aggregate-after-contrast FDID sensitivity interval equals the sharp cell-level moderation LP, and strict conservative and anti-conservative witnesses when it fails.

Refute the scalar post-contrast shortcut for FDID moderation sensitivity by showing shared imbalance coordinates must be pooled before support evaluation and providing the corrected pooled-coordinate interval.

Represent the FDID moderation identified set as a support-function body generated by shared moderator-stratum imbalance coordinates rather than by coordinatewise boxes, and characterize when the body is non-rectangular.
Jointly identify effect modification, causal moderation bounds, and the sign-robustness frontier from one finite completion polytope rather than treating the three objects separately.
Find the smallest positive rational imbalance budget for which an explicit 2x2 FDID table yields opposite signs for effect modification and feasible causal moderation.
Extend the bounded-imbalance FDID moderation interval to covariate-conditional strata and show the pooled-coordinate support still beats separate stratumwise subtraction.

Characterize the one-sided design in which one moderator stratum obeys exact factorial trends and only the other stratum carries imbalance, yielding a half-width moderation interval with closed-form sign frontier.
Bound the distortion gap tau_em(P)-theta_cm itself as the primitive FDID sensitivity object and compare its sharp interval to direct moderation intervals.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the sharp finite-cell LP and dual support interval for FDID causal moderation when factorial parallel trends is relaxed to a bounded moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance budget, collapsing to XuZhaoDing2026FDID at zero imbalance.

anchor_paper.author: Yiqing Xu, Anqi Zhao, Peng Ding
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Standard FDID assumptions identify effect modification but not causal moderation; the latter needs an additional factorial parallel-trends condition.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the DID estimator identifies effect modification but not causal moderation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M3+M4, not a routine relaxation: the surprise content is the pooled-coordinate LP geometry and zero-budget recovery, not merely wider bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends plus BalkePearl1997Bounds because bounded trend violations with a linear target always give an LP interval.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: neither paper instantiates the FDID moderation target or the shared moderator-stratum completion map, and the proposed gap is the pooled-coordinate FDID-specific support formula.

adversarial_referee.instance: Need a named focal object beyond 'bounds'.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the focal object is the FDID moderation support interval Theta_I(P;rho) with dual support map h_P(c;rho).

adversarial_referee.instance: Xu may already imply the positive-budget formula in remarks.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the paper states the point-ID distinction and extra assumption; the positive-budget finite-cell LP is not stated there.

adversarial_referee.instance: LP sufficiency without sharpness is incremental.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the headline conjecture is explicitly a sharpness claim for the full FDID problem, not only a feasible outer LP.

seed_details.one_line: Define the critical imbalance scalar rho^-(P) for FDID and characterize exactly when a positive effect-modification contrast can coexist with negative causal moderation on an open class of finite tables.

anchor_paper.author: Yiqing Xu, Anqi Zhao, Peng Ding
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: FDID distinguishes effect modification from causal moderation in panel/factorial DiD.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: define effect modification and causal moderation as the associative and causal effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M3+M8: stronger than a generic sign example because it names a critical scalar and asks for an open-set sign-reversal region, not a lone witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a corollary of the LP interval and does not deserve a separate flagship theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only if the LP support is already available; here the frontier rho^-(P) is the operational scalar a user would report, and the open-set sign-reversal claim is extra substance.

adversarial_referee.instance: Need one scalar readers can compute.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: rho^-(P) is named explicitly as the smallest common imbalance budget causing sign reversal.

adversarial_referee.instance: Single hand-tuned table may be trivial.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the seed promises open-class promotion beyond the worked rational table.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could also report zero-entry frontier and width frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: accepted; theorem can be extended later, but the sign frontier is the clean first object because it answers the moderation-vs-effect-modification dispute directly.

seed_details.one_line: Give a necessary-and-sufficient equality certificate for when an aggregate-after-contrast FDID sensitivity interval equals the sharp cell-level moderation LP, and strict conservative and anti-conservative witnesses when it fails.

anchor_paper.author: prior_proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Aggregation can change the sensitivity object before inference begins.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: when aggregation changes the sensitivity object before inference begins

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M7+M4: stronger than 'aggregate bounds are conservative' because it asks for an iff certificate and both conservative and anti-conservative failures.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is the same aggregation-vs-cell sensitivity warning already explored in panel_honest_did_phase_v1.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that banked run concerned cohort/event-study Honest-DiD geometry, not the FDID moderation quotient or its primitive equality certificate.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: necessity must be part of the seed; otherwise downgrade to field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could end up only showing strict inequality examples.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: if equality side stalls, downgrade; as written it is an iff seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Aggregate interval comparator may target another object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the proposal must define both scalar shortcut and cell LP on the same moderation target before claiming equality or failure.

seed_details.one_line: Refute the scalar post-contrast shortcut for FDID moderation sensitivity by showing shared imbalance coordinates must be pooled before support evaluation and providing the corrected pooled-coordinate interval.

anchor_paper.author: prior_proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Shared latent coordinates must be summed before absolute values are taken.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: summing signed coefficients before taking absolute values

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M1+M3: stronger than a generic sensitivity extension because it diagnoses a tempting shortcut and replaces it with a corrected interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just an adaptation of pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 from anticipation to moderation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly true, so this is not the chosen flagship seed; the novelty ceiling is lower because the geometry is reusable from the anticipation proposal.

seed_details.one_line: Represent the FDID moderation identified set as a support-function body generated by shared moderator-stratum imbalance coordinates rather than by coordinatewise boxes, and characterize when the body is non-rectangular.

anchor_paper.author: Ashesh Rambachan, Jonathan Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Parallel-trends violations can be handled by partial identification under explicit restrictions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: The causal parameter of interest is partially identified under these restrictions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11: non-rectangularity is stronger than 'bounds widen' because the support body itself is the focal object.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from any LP image of a polytope and does not need FDID.
seed_details.rebuttal: Mostly true unless tied to a comparator chain against coordinatewise FDID shortcuts; scored below the main LP interval seed.

seed_details.one_line: Jointly identify effect modification, causal moderation bounds, and the sign-robustness frontier from one finite completion polytope rather than treating the three objects separately.

anchor_paper.author: Yiqing Xu, Anqi Zhao, Peng Ding
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: The FDID paper identifies one target under standard assumptions and distinguishes it from causal moderation.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: under standard assumptions, including no anticipation and parallel trends

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6: stronger than a one-estimand seed because one primitive completion object would simultaneously deliver tau_em, Theta_I, and rho^-.
seed_details.referee_objection: The family is too small; joint identification here may be packaging, not substance.
seed_details.rebuttal: Fair criticism; this is a secondary seed unless the family can be widened to include additional moderation functionals.

seed_details.one_line: Find the smallest positive rational imbalance budget for which an explicit 2x2 FDID table yields opposite signs for effect modification and feasible causal moderation.

anchor_paper.author: Kirk Bansak
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series A
anchor_paper.claim: Treatment-effect heterogeneity is not enough for causal moderation.
anchor_paper.location: section 2, lines 88-99 on the OUP page
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: finding treatment effect heterogeneity is not sufficient; what is needed is the estimation of causal moderation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M3 diagnostic only: stronger if paired with a threshold scalar; weaker if left as a lone table.
seed_details.referee_objection: A single smallest rational witness is cute but field-tier unless generalized.
seed_details.rebuttal: Agreed; useful as an exhibit or corollary, not as the flagship kernel by itself.

seed_details.one_line: Extend the bounded-imbalance FDID moderation interval to covariate-conditional strata and show the pooled-coordinate support still beats separate stratumwise subtraction.

anchor_paper.author: prior_proposal
anchor_paper.claim: A common latent path can perturb multiple weighted targets jointly.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: same latent post-treatment trend discrepancy path perturbs every weighted aggregate simultaneously

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension from unconditional to conditional is weaker unless the pooled-support gain survives conditioning; still likely field-tier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine conditioning refinement once the unconditional interval exists.
seed_details.rebuttal: Mostly true; not suitable as the opening angle unless the conditional geometry changes qualitatively.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the one-sided design in which one moderator stratum obeys exact factorial trends and only the other stratum carries imbalance, yielding a half-width moderation interval with closed-form sign frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Charles F. Manski, John V. Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Zero tolerance recovers invariance-style conclusions while positive tolerance yields ambiguity sets.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Our bounded variation assumptions weaken in various respects the invariance assumptions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift seed; stronger if the one-sided design reveals a phase change, weaker if it is only a simplification for exposition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a special case corollary of the general LP interval.
seed_details.rebuttal: Agreed; useful as a sanity corollary or computational subclass, not as the main flagship angle.

seed_details.one_line: Bound the distortion gap tau_em(P)-theta_cm itself as the primitive FDID sensitivity object and compare its sharp interval to direct moderation intervals.

anchor_paper.author: Thomas Demuynck
anchor_paper.claim: Linear restrictions can be turned into a simple LP bound problem.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: obtain bounds on the mean treatment effects by solving a simple linear programming problem

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: New-object framing only if the distortion gap itself is shown to be the operational quantity users should report; otherwise it is a reparameterization.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a relabeling of the moderation interval around tau_em.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely true unless the distortion object has cleaner dual geometry or sharper consumer interpretation.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Xu, Zhao, and Ding (2026, JASA) is the anchor: FDID treats a baseline factor G and exposure Z as a factorial DiD design.
2. Their central identification result says standard FDID assumptions identify effect modification, not causal moderation.
3. Causal moderation requires an additional factorial/generalized parallel-trends mean-independence condition.
4. Bansak (2021, JRSS A) and VanderWeele (2009, Epidemiology) supply the broader distinction between subgroup heterogeneity/effect modification and causal moderation/interaction.
5. Rambachan and Roth (2023, ReStud) provide the closest DiD sensitivity template: bounded deviations from parallel trends yield partial identification.
6. Manski and Pepper (2018, ReStat) provide a bounded-variation precedent where zero tolerance recovers point restrictions and positive tolerance yields ambiguity sets.
7. Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA) and Demuynck (2015, Economics Letters) justify finite LP sharp-bounds technology for discrete causal problems with linear restrictions.
8. Robust DiD bounds papers such as Ban and Kedagni are adjacent, but target treatment effects under selection/trend deviations rather than FDID causal moderation.
9. The web scan found no published result giving sharp FDID causal-moderation bounds under moderator-stratum untreated-trend imbalance.
10. The scan also found no published finite FDID witness where effect modification and causal moderation have opposite signs.
11. The live tension is therefore between a point-ID FDID moderation theorem at zero imbalance and the partial-ID/sensitivity literature for nonzero trend deviations.
12. Study-pipeline interval-inference gaps are adjacent for later inference work, but the current topic is the identification and sharpness substrate.

banked_reason: Interrupted during Stage -0.5 after three consecutive REVISE reviews at field tier: the finite FDID moderation-bounds topic had a real literature gap but the reviewer kept blocking flagship status on remaining novelty/correctness issues, so it was stopped before burning the full pivot budget.
